title: NOC 40010 – Government Managers – Health and Social Policy Development and Program Administration | GTR Immigration
description: Government Managers – Health and Social Policy Development and Program Administration under NOC 40010 are senior public administration leaders responsible for shaping health and social policies across Canada. As Canada continues expanding healthcare systems, immigration services, housing programs, and social support initiatives, experienced policy managers remain in steady demand under this TEER 0 management occupation. For qualified professionals, NOC 40010 offers strong eligibility under Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker Program) and selected Provincial Nominee Program streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services, and in public protection servicesSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection servicesMinor Group4001 – Managers in public administrationNOC Code40010

common_job_titles: City medical officer of health – government services
Health information and promotion director – government services
Health program operations manager – government services
Housing policy and development director – government services
Immigration and settlement director – government services
Social services planning manager – government services
duties: Government Managers in Health and Social Policy Development perform some or all of the following:
Develop and advise on public health or social policy
Organize departmental units and establish operational procedures
Direct policy researchers, consultants, and program officers
Ensure compliance with federal and provincial legislation
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in health sciences, hospital administration, public administration, or social sciences
Several years of experience as policy researcher, consultant, or program officer
Professional certification required in certain roles (e.g., city medical officers must be licensed physicians)
Bachelor’s degree in sociology, economics, public administration, or related discipline
Several years of experience in policy research or administration
Public service roles may require security clearance and bilingual proficiency in English and French depending on the level of government.
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range from $64,900 to $148,000, depending on jurisdiction, seniority, and specialization.
job_outlook: Social housing and community development initiatives
Senior-level experience and graduate-level qualifications may significantly enhance employment competitiveness.
labour_market_demand: Government Managers in this category are employed at:
Demand is strongest in major provinces including Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, Nova Scotia, and northern jurisdictions such as Nunavut and Northwest Territories.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 40010?
Strong job security in public administration
Career advancement within federal and provincial systems
This occupation represents one of the highest-skilled pathways within Canada’s public sector management.
